replies.msg: Textbook 今日學AS

Denote a as the 1st term, d as the common difference, Kn as the general term.
Kn = (n/2)(2a + (n-1)d)
= an + (nd/2)(n-1)
= (d/2)n^2 + (a-(d/2))n
After that the result can be obtained trivially by inputting the correct values for a and d.
Also one can use this result to obtain a and d from Kn for multiple choice questions.

replies.msg:
(a)
Note that 7^(4n) ≡ 1 (mod 10) for n is a non-negative integer
1997^2036 ≡ 7^2036 ≡ 7^(4(509)) ≡ 1 (mod 10)
So the last digit of 1997^2036 is 1

Alternatively, consider 1997^2036 = (2000 - 3)^2036.
The first digit of 1997^2036 is the last digit of 3^2036
Note that the last digit of 3^(4n) is 1 for n is a non-negative integer
3^2036 = 3^(4(509))
So the last digit of 1997^2036 is 1
